replies.author: @davidt1621
replies.text:
For the conspiracy theorist tin-foil-hat-wearers out there always going on about "fake meat" and other gross ideas that can easily be disproven:
Your average feedlot cow produces around 200-300 pounds of hamburger meat after being slaughtered and ground up into patties. That's smaller cows on the low end, and most live long enough to make 300+pounds of hamburger meat. Multiply that 300 average by 6 to get the number of 1/6th-pound patties, which is the size used in your regular MickeyD's basic little hamburger. That's 1800 of those little hamburgers from one cow. Multiply that by a typical 1,000 cow feedlot, and you get 1,800,000 burgers. There's hundreds of those feedlots all over the southwestern USA. THAT'S where they come from. THERE'S the REAL numbers! Next time a conspiracy theorist says something that sounds wild, do your own fact-checking before spreading ignorance.

This ain't a war, it's straight up bullying. 😂

This has been going on for years, for anyone who remembers.
McDonald's makes a special promo, come off as disingenuous, fail miserably and gets backlash, Burger King mocks them by doing almost the same thing, just better, McDonald's go back into their shell, and a few years later muster up the courage to repeat the whole thing over again.

The problem is, McDonald's is so separated from their customer base and the common man that they have no idea why they failed and just repeat the same mistake over and over again. Meanwhile, Burger King knows marketing better, knows what the common man doesn't like to see, and also knows that roasting your competition gets positive feedback, so they get a boost every time McDonald's musters up their courage again. 😂
